en: Either of you can answer. I'm sorry, sir. I thought you were reading the letter. I've read the letter.
ko: 둘 중 한 명이 대답해도 돼요.

en: Well, we came up with an idea for a website called Harvard Connection
ko: 저희는 하버드 커넥션이라는 웹사이트 아이디어를 생각해냈습니다.

en: that we've since changed the name to connect to you, and Mark Zuckerberg stole that idea.
ko: 그래서 제가 그에 대해 무엇을 해주길 원하시는 건가요?

en: I understand, and I'm asking what you want me to do about it.
Well, sir, in the Harvard Student Handbook, which is distributed to each freshman
under the heading Standards of Conduct in the Harvard Community, it says the college expects all students
ko: 대학은 모든 학생이 이 커뮤니티의 구성원들과의 거래에서

en: to be honest and forthcoming in their dealings with members in this community.
ko: 학생들은 공공 및 사유 재산을 존중해야 하며,

en: Students are required to respect public and private ownership and instances of theft, misappropriation...
Anne. Yes, sir? Punch me in the face. Go ahead.
Or unauthorized use will result in disciplinary action, including the requirement to withdraw from the college.
ko: 무단 사용은 징계 조치를 초래할 수 있으며,

en: You memorized that instead of doing what? What my brother and I came here today to ask of you,
ko: 당신은 그걸 외우고 대신 무엇을 했나요?

en: respectfully, of course, is it's against university rules to steal from another student, plain and simple.
ko: 물론 존중하는 마음으로, 다른 학생에게서 훔치는 것은 대학 규칙에 위배된다는 것입니다. 간단합니다.

en: And you've spoken to your housemaster? Yes, sir.
ko: 그리고 당신은 하우스 마스터와 이야기했나요? 네, 선생님.
